# UC-23.9.1 · Enterprise GHG Inventory: Scopes 1–3 CO2e with Dual Scope 2 and Factor Lookup

> **Criticality:** Critical &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Platform &middot; **Type:** Compliance, Business &middot; **Wave:** Crawl &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We add up pollution from company trucks and boilers, purchased power using both grid-average and green-contract math, and business flights together so bosses see one credible climate footprint—not piecemeal guesses split across spreadsheets.*

---

## Description

Chains monthly-close activity quantities from metered electricity (two deliberate Scope 2 branches for **location-based** grid-average intensity versus **market-based** supplier/REC/PPA factors), stationary natural gas by volume, diesel fleet litres from procurement JDBC pulls, and Scope 3 Category 6 passenger-kilometres from corporate travel extracts—each arm labelled by GHG Protocol Scope—then applies kgCO2e per native activity unit from `carbon_factors.csv` so Splunk emits reconciled tonnes CO2e by Scope and source for CSRD/ESRS E1-style climate disclosures, CDP Climate, SBTi progress narratives, and ISSB IFRS S2-aligned management commentary without collapsing Scope boundaries.

## Value

Chief sustainability officers, group controllers, and disclosure-committee sponsors gain auditable linkage between operational telemetry (facilities indexes), finance-grade procurement rows (business indexes via DB Connect), and annually stewarded emission-factor tables—supporting ISO 14064-3 assurance loops (DNV, Bureau Veritas, SGS-class reviewers expect documented dual Scope 2 plus Scope boundary notes). Net-zero and science-based-target storylines improve because Splunk separates structural savings from inventory-method noise: leaders see whether tonnes dropped because renewable procurement retired credible REC volumes versus grid-average intensity revisions alone—essential when residual-mix disclosure sits beside location-based totals expected by regulators.

## Implementation

Stand up HEC tokens for BMS-style JSON meter feeds into `facilities`; configure DB Connect inputs (`cron_schedule` aligned to nightly procurement close) emitting fleet and travel staging sourcetypes into `business`; publish `carbon_factors.csv` with version columns and governance workflow for BEIS/DEFRA/eGRID annual swaps; paste the canonical SPL into a scheduled monthly saved search writing board-ready totals plus optional summary index for YoY overlays.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites
- Splunk Enterprise 9.2 or newer or Splunk Cloud with roles granting access to index=facilities, index=business, and lookups on search heads used by Sustainability and FP&A personas.
- HTTP Event Collector tokens scoped per site or aggregator forwarder class for JSON payloads {site,meter_id,kwh_consumed,timestamp}; prefer Splunk Cloud-managed tokens or indexer acknowledgement patterns consistent with five-to-fifteen-minute cadence expectations.
- Splunk DB Connect Splunkbase app 2686 on a Heavy Forwarder or Cloud bulk-ingest tier with JDBC connectivity to procurement databases exposing diesel litres by transaction id plus travel staging rows keyed by traveller segment.
- CSV lookup carbon_factors.csv (minimum columns source, kg_co2e_per_activity_unit, activity_unit, optional methodology_note, factor_year) deployed via Settings → Lookups → Lookup table files with transforms.conf stanza naming lookup carbon_factors, or mirrored KV Store collection carbon_factors when auditors demand immutable versioning alongside Splunk searches referencing inputlookup append=t refreshes.
- Governance memo naming consolidation approach (operational control versus financial control versus equity-share per GHG Protocol Corporate Standard chapters), baseline fiscal year lock for Science Based Targets initiative milestones, and locked dual-reporting posture for Scope 2 location-based grid-average versus market-based contractual instruments.

**Step 1 — Configure data collection**
Wire sourcetype="power_meter" into index=facilities first because Scope 2 electricity dominates many inventories: normalize JSON keys such as kwh_consumed, guard divide-by-zero later with coalesce, and stamp _time from meter timestamps—not indexer receipt—to preserve monthly-close alignment using earliest=-1mon@mon latest=@mon. For Scope 2 market-based reporting, procurement must publish supplier-specific kgCO2e per kWh either merged upstream into duplicate meter rows tagged reporting_basis=market or maintained as distinct lookup rows keyed electricity_market_based; residual mix rows come from supplier disclosures when power purchase agreements lack bespoke factors.
Scope 1 stationary gas ingestion requires volumetric fidelity using cubic_metres_natural_gas with documented conversion from hourly BMS volumetric pulses—avoid silently multiplying by arbitrary energy-density constants unless identical constants appear in carbon_factors.csv methodology notes referencing BEIS stationary combustion guidance.
Fleet diesel litres arrive via DB Connect scheduled inputs (cron_schedule illustration only—tune after observing ERP posting latency). Parameterized SQL pulls incremental litres since watermark column last_modified; sourcetypes remain stable while dialect differs—examples jdbc_fleet_fuel versus rs_coupa_fuel_txn as referenced in the canonical SPL.
Travel extracts sourcetype="rs_travel_air_rail" should aggregate passenger-kilometres already multiplied by airline-specific routing factors inside SQL when possible—Splunk stays declarative Scope 3 Category 6 totals rather than rebuilding ICAO calculators unless SME-approved SPL forks exist elsewhere.
Annual lookup stewardship: publish scheduled alert comparing checksum of uploaded CSV versus prior revision when Sustainability uploads revised BEIS factors—June publication cadence implies organisations issuing EU Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive compliant statements before refresh continue prior-year coefficients deliberately; annotate factor_year column accordingly.

**Step 2 — Create the search and emissions ledger**
Paste the authoritative SPL string from the spl field into a saved search named ghg_inventory_monthly_dual_scope2. Technical intent per GHG Protocol inventory principles: each append branch emits homogeneous tuples (site, scope, source) plus activity_qty expressed in the native activity unit documented beside its lookup row (kWh, cubic metres, litres, passenger-kilometres). After unioning branches, lookup carbon_factors.csv source OUTPUT kg_co2e_per_activity_unit methodology_note factor_year attaches authoritative coefficients—multiply quantity by coefficient and divide one thousand because corporate reporting expresses mass as tonnes carbon dioxide equivalent while national factor tables publish kilograms.
Explicit duplication of metered kilowatt-hours under both electricity_location_based and electricity_market_based keys intentionally counts electrical activity twice across methodologies—not twice across physics—because GHG Protocol mandates presenting both inventories separately; dashboards must label charts accordingly.
Guardrail coalesce(kg_co2e_per_activity_unit,0) prevents null explosions during pilots but zeros silently mask missing lookup rows—promote interim alert when coefficient null-rate exceeds tolerance.
Optional refinement for enterprises needing ERP site taxonomy drift: prepend each branch with eval site=coalesce(site,facility,location,"Unknown") already shown—extend with lookup site_region.csv site OUTPUT grid_region feeding derivative sources such as electricity_location_based_us_rfc if CSV granularity exceeds Splunk appetite.
Secondary year-on-year posture uses appendcols wrapping prior-year identical month windows referencing locked baseline totals stored either as literal constants table climate_baselines.csv or KV Store keyed by scenario—defer elaborate appendcols choreography to sibling dashboards once monthly ledger proves trustworthy.

**Step 3 — Validate**
Verify against the Schneider EcoStruxure site dashboard totals that rolling monthly kilowatt-hours reconcile with Splunk sums sourced from sourcetype power_meter within nominal rounding tolerance—mirror the same exercise against Siemens Desigo CC energy summaries where those gateways feed meters.
Reconcile Splunk tonnes from the location-based Scope 2 branch against the same grid-intensity arithmetic your CDP verifier signs off: first compare monthly sum(kwh_consumed) from sourcetype="power_meter" with the Schneider EcoStruxure Power Manager or Siemens Desigo CC site energy dashboard export for two pilot locations (variance typically under two percent once time zones align)—this exercise satisfies auditors who expect a vendor-console cross-check alongside Splunk-side rollups.
Cross-check diesel litres branch totals inside Splunk against the Coupa Analytics commodity fuel report UI filtered to diesel grades and identical site hierarchy—quantity mismatches typically arise when duplicate JDBC pulls replay settled invoices; enforce incremental watermark discipline on the DB Connect job.
Validate Scope 3 Category 6 passenger-kilometres against the Egencia or Concur Insights trip report for a sample employee cohort versus Splunk staging—mismatched airline coding ruins passenger-kilometres versus airport-distance shortcuts; open the airline itinerary web portal for the same bookings if doubt remains.
Market-based Scope 2 requires parity between Splunk tonne outputs and procurement-held Renewable Energy Certificate retirement schedules documented in spreadsheet retirements tracker—Finance confirms REC serial counts versus billed megawatt-hours before auditors rely on Splunk charts.
Finally compare Splunk headline totals against provisional greenhouse gas inventory workbook compiled under ISO 14064-1 hierarchy—differences exceeding threshold trigger Sustainability steering-committee variance investigation queue.

**Step 4 — Operationalize**
Publish Dashboard Studio workspace tabs separating dual Scope 2 charts from Scope 1 stacks—colour-blind-safe palettes mandated by ISSB investor-facing readability norms—and annotate methodology revisions as vertical markers whenever Sustainability uploads factor-year increments.
Schedule monthly saved search firing email digest with tonnes CO2e table attachments routed to Sustainability inbox plus optional Slack webhook.
Embed governance footer citing consolidation boundary memo revision IDs.
Escalate alerts only after verifying ingestion staleness detectors using metadata searches confirming _indextime minus _time skew thresholds per sourcetype so transient Splunk delays never declare emissions crises falsely.
Role-based ACL segregation grants Sustainability editors lookup-write capability via Knowledge Manager privilege separate from FP&A readers consuming dashboards alone.
Archive monthly Splunk PDF exports plus CSV lookup snapshots into controlled SharePoint retention labelled ISO 14064 evidence bundles matching reviewer sampling expectations.

**Step 5 — Troubleshooting**
- Missing fields after the lookup-driven stats aggregation usually indicate stale transforms.conf definitions or CSV headers drifting relative to transforms lookup_field expectations—reload lookups under Settings → Lookups → Lookup definitions after Sustainability uploads revised carbon_factors.csv rows.
- Zero tonnes across Scope 2 branches despite nonzero kwh_consumed rows: search for missing kg_co2e_per_activity_unit after the lookup step—misnamed lookup keys such as electricity_location_base instead of electricity_location_based produce null coefficients and coalesce zeros the product; run the append branch standalone and compare key names to carbon_factors.csv line by line.
- HTTP Event Collector token mis-issuance returns HTTP 403 on posts to services/collector/event while _internal logs show TcpInputProcessor errors—rotate the token under Settings → Data inputs → HTTP Event Collector and update the forwarder stanza within five minutes so power_meter events resume before the monthly close misses data.
- DB Connect JDBC jobs show SQLException ORA-12170 or Snowflake JDBC timeout after 300 seconds—narrow SQL predicates, raise fetchSize gradually, or shard supplier extracts so jdbc_fleet_fuel commits finish within scheduler SLAs.
- Duplicate JDBC replay duplicates litres—duplicate transaction_id warnings belong upstream SQL using SELECT DISTINCT—Splunk symptom saturates Scope 1 tonnes until checkpoints reset on the Identity tab inside DB Connect inputs.
- Append-heavy latency spikes indicate branch cardinality exploding—collapse each branch stats earlier before union—prefer summary indexes once Splunk monitoring confirms stable ingestion volumes approaching fifty thousand candidate rows before aggregation.
- _time drift versus billed utility windows arises when meter timestamps omit daylight-saving conversions—normalize using Splunk-configured time_zone per sourcetype before monthly earliest macros.
- Travel CSV ingestion latency manifests as abrupt Scope 3 troughs coinciding with frozen expense reimbursement portals—coordinate blackout commentary within quarterly storytelling until sourcetype="rs_travel_air_rail" resumes posting rows with nonzero passenger_km.
- Methodology divergence comparing Splunk totals versus ISO workbook arises when Splunk retains operational-control stance while workbook adopts equity-share consolidation mid-cycle—freeze memo governance synchronously with disclosure-committee approval dates.
- Refrigerant fugitive estimation gaps persist—Splunk seldom replaces EPA leak-rate proxies until HVAC contractor uploads leak volumes—never promise completeness beyond instrumentation investments Sustainable Facilities sponsors explicitly fund.

## SPL

```spl
index=facilities sourcetype="power_meter" earliest=-1mon@mon latest=@mon
| eval site=coalesce(site,facility,location,"Unknown")
| stats sum(kwh_consumed) as activity_qty by site
| eval scope="Scope_2", source="electricity_location_based"
| append [
    search index=facilities sourcetype="power_meter" earliest=-1mon@mon latest=@mon
    | eval site=coalesce(site,facility,location,"Unknown")
    | stats sum(kwh_consumed) as activity_qty by site
    | eval scope="Scope_2", source="electricity_market_based"
  ]
| append [
    search index=facilities sourcetype="gas_meter" earliest=-1mon@mon latest=@mon
    | eval site=coalesce(site,facility,location,"Unknown")
    | stats sum(cubic_metres_natural_gas) as activity_qty by site
    | eval scope="Scope_1", source="natural_gas_stationary"
  ]
| append [
    search index=business (sourcetype="jdbc_fleet_fuel" OR sourcetype="rs_coupa_fuel_txn") earliest=-1mon@mon latest=@mon
    | eval site=coalesce(site,cost_center_site,location,"Unknown")
    | stats sum(litres_diesel) as activity_qty by site
    | eval scope="Scope_1", source="fleet_diesel_on_road"
  ]
| append [
    search index=business sourcetype="rs_travel_air_rail" earliest=-1mon@mon latest=@mon
    | eval site=coalesce(billing_site,employee_home_country_site,"Unknown")
    | stats sum(passenger_km) as activity_qty by site
    | eval scope="Scope_3", source="business_travel_cat6_air_domestic"
  ]
| lookup carbon_factors.csv source OUTPUT kg_co2e_per_activity_unit methodology_note factor_year
| eval tonnes_co2e=round(activity_qty * coalesce(kg_co2e_per_activity_unit,0) / 1000, 4)
| stats sum(tonnes_co2e) as total_tonnes_co2e first(methodology_note) as methodology_note first(factor_year) as factor_year by scope, source
| eval total_tonnes_co2e=round(total_tonnes_co2e, 3)
| sort scope, source
| table scope, source, total_tonnes_co2e, methodology_note, factor_year
```

## Visualization

Dashboard Studio or Classic XML: stacked column chart of tonnes CO2e by **Scope** (1 vs 2 vs 3 Cat 6); donut or trellis pie breaking **source** within Scope (electricity LB vs MB vs gas vs diesel vs travel); time-series line overlay of rolling-twelve-month tonnes CO2e versus science-based trajectory with annotated methodology-change markers; headline single-value panel for portfolio tonnes CO2e plus secondary single-value for percentage reduction versus locked baseline fiscal year.

## Known False Positives

Totals swing after the annual BEIS/DESNZ June conversion-factor workbook replaces last year’s coefficients while CDP questionnaires mid-cycle still instruct reporters to freeze factors until fiscal-year governance votes—Splunk shows movement analysts mis-label as operational drift. Regional grid-average intensities shift when ENTSO-E or EPA eGRID rebaseline sub-region boundaries (RFC versus your utility footprint versus balancing-authority splits), inflating apparent variance unrelated to onsite behaviour. Legacy GHG Protocol Scope 2 Guidance dual-reporting versus ISO 14064-1 consolidation clauses yields conflicting tonnes when procurement insists market-based residual mix imports lag supplier certificates by a quarter. Scope 3 Category 6 spreadsheet proxies sometimes approximate rail legs as generic passenger-km factors while traveller itineraries mix international segments requiring ICAO-tier splits—Splunk faithfully totals whichever simplistic keys you coded. Operational-control consolidation captures entire leased-building Scope 1 gas while equity-share reporters expect proration—inventory spikes near lease classification reviews without operational change. Acquisition doubles Scope 1 diesel exposure mid-year unless you apply contractual cut-over dates aligned with acquisition accounting teams; divestitures symmetrically deflate totals in ways YoY dashboards miscount as savings. Carbon-offset retirement timestamps rarely align with meter-month attributions—finance books tonnes retired in December against November operational stacks—Splunk surfaces temporal mismatch risk under scrutiny. Renewable Energy Certificate procurement teams occasionally retire more certificates than megawatt-hours justified by billed meters—market-based Scope 2 rows risk optimism bias unless procurement confirms REC serial linkage per invoice month. Diesel litres accidentally sourced with imperial gallons rather than litres silently distort tonnes until fleet masters reconcile tank throughput conversions—conversion-factor drift hides inside procurement staging joins (fleet diesel-vs-electric mix migrations amplify uncertainty once electrification percentages shift quarter-to-quarter). Business-travel extracts sourced from expense reimbursement batches lag ticket flown-date by four-to-eight weeks—near-real-time Sustainability KPI commentary diverges from SAP Concur/Egencia CSV snapshots Splunk indexes nightly.
